pr.title: Fixed Full Scan graph crash on mixed-polarity transition lists (#4240)
pr.body: ## Summary * `GraphFullScan.CreateSingleScan` now skips transitions whose polarity differs from the displayed scan instead of asserting — fixes the `AssumptionException` in exception report #74770. * Added `MixedPolarityFullScanTest`: a regression test that builds a `ScanProvider` whose transition list spans both polarities and opens the Full Scan on a positive scan. A plain UI click can't reproduce it (opposite-polarity precursors always render in separate panes), so the test constructs the condition directly via `ShowGraphFullScan`. * Added a scan **Polarity** row to the Full Scan Acquisition property sheet (useful for mixed-polarity data), and made "True"/"False" localizable for "Is Centroided" display. This is the only user-facing change. Fixes #4240 ## Test plan - [x] TestMixedPolarityFullScan — fails with `AssumptionException` (GraphFullScan.cs:1951) without the fix, passes with it - [x] TestFullScanGraph — passes with the new Polarity property Co-Authored-By: Claude
pr.author: bspratt
